definition_en: To do or say something to relieve tension or get a conversation started
definition_pt: Fazer ou dizer algo para aliviar a tensão ou iniciar uma conversa
example: He told a joke to break the ice at the meeting.

definition_en: Very easy to do
definition_pt: Muito fácil de fazer
example: The test was as easy as pie.

definition_en: It is better to do something late than not at all
definition_pt: É melhor fazer algo tarde do que nunca fazer
example: He finally apologized - better late than never.

definition_en: Good luck
definition_pt: Boa sorte
example: You have a performance tonight? Break a leg!

definition_en: To stop what you are doing for the rest of the night
definition_pt: Parar o que você está fazendo pelo resto da noite
example: It's getting late - let's call it a night.

definition_en: To attract someone's attention
definition_pt: Atrair a atenção de alguém
example: The bright colors caught my eye.

definition_en: To change your opinion or decision
definition_pt: Mudar sua opinião ou decisão
example: I was going to stay home, but I changed my mind.

definition_en: Extremely clean
definition_pt: Extremamente limpo
example: The kitchen is clean as a whistle.

definition_en: To happen as expected or wished
definition_pt: Acontecer como esperado ou desejado
example: Her dream of becoming a doctor came true.

definition_en: Someone who watches a lot of television
definition_pt: Alguém que assiste muita televisão
example: Stop being a couch potato and go outside!

definition_en: To be upset about something that has already happened
definition_pt: Ficar chateado com algo que já aconteceu
example: It's too late to change it - don't cry over spilled milk.

definition_en: Something that you like or enjoy
definition_pt: Algo que você gosta ou aprecia
example: Horror movies are not my cup of tea.

definition_en: To help someone
definition_pt: Ajudar alguém
example: Could you do me a favor and turn down the music?

definition_en: Practical and realistic
definition_pt: Prático e realista
example: I like her because she's very down to earth.

definition_en: To annoy someone very much
definition_pt: Irritar alguém muito
example: That loud music is driving me crazy!

definition_en: To write a short message to someone
definition_pt: Escrever uma mensagem curta para alguém
example: Drop me a line when you arrive home.

translation: mais fácil falar do que fazer
definition_en: More difficult than it seems
definition_pt: Mais difícil do que parece
example: Finding a good job is easier said than done.

definition_en: To feel wonderful or great
definition_pt: Sentir-se maravilhoso ou ótimo
example: After the vacation, I feel like a million bucks.

definition_en: To really enjoy something
definition_pt: Realmente gostar de algo
example: I get a kick out of watching funny videos.

definition_en: To have a good relationship with someone
definition_pt: Ter um bom relacionamento com alguém
example: I get along well with my coworkers.

definition_en: To become nervous about doing something
definition_pt: Ficar nervoso sobre fazer algo
example: He got cold feet before the wedding.

definition_en: To talk about something that has been bothering you
definition_pt: Falar sobre algo que tem te incomodado
example: I need to get this off my chest - I'm sorry.

definition_en: To start something or get something started
definition_pt: Começar algo ou fazer algo começar
example: Let's get the ball rolling on this project.

definition_en: To ignore someone on purpose
definition_pt: Ignorar alguém de propósito
example: She gave me the cold shoulder after our argument.

expression: go back to the drawing board
definition_en: To start over from the beginning
definition_pt: Recomeçar do início
example: Our plan failed - it's back to the drawing board.

definition_en: To fail completely
definition_pt: Fracassar completamente
example: His presentation went down in flames.

definition_en: To make extra effort
definition_pt: Fazer um esforço extra
example: Good employees go the extra mile for customers.

definition_en: To do what others are doing
definition_pt: Fazer o que os outros estão fazendo
example: I don't have a plan - I just go with the flow.

expression: good things come to those who wait
definition_en: Patient people will be rewarded
definition_pt: Pessoas pacientes serão recompensadas
example: Be patient - good things come to those who wait.

definition_en: To change your feelings or opinion
definition_pt: Mudar seus sentimentos ou opinião
example: She had a change of heart and accepted the job.

definition_en: To like sugary foods
definition_pt: Gostar de alimentos açucarados
example: I have a sweet tooth - I love chocolate cake.

definition_en: Deeply in love
definition_pt: Profundamente apaixonado
example: He's head over heels in love with her.

expression: hear something straight from the horse's mouth
definition_en: To hear something directly from the person involved
definition_pt: Ouvir algo diretamente da pessoa envolvida
example: I heard it straight from the horse's mouth - he's quitting.

definition_en: To start a journey
definition_pt: Começar uma jornada
example: We should hit the road before it gets dark.

definition_en: To wait and be patient
definition_pt: Esperar e ser paciente
example: Hold your horses! We're not ready yet.

definition_en: In a few words; briefly
definition_pt: Em poucas palavras; brevemente
example: In a nutshell, the project was a success.

definition_en: In the same difficult situation
definition_pt: Na mesma situação difícil
example: We both lost our jobs - we're in the same boat.

translation: é preciso dois para dançar tango
definition_en: Both people involved are responsible
definition_pt: Ambas as pessoas envolvidas são responsáveis
example: Don't blame only him - it takes two to tango.

definition_en: To do something too early
definition_pt: Fazer algo muito cedo
example: Let's not jump the gun until we know the facts.

definition_en: To watch or monitor something
definition_pt: Observar ou monitorar algo
example: Can you keep an eye on my bag for a minute?

definition_en: To stay positive in a difficult situation
definition_pt: Manter-se positivo em uma situação difícil
example: Keep your chin up - things will get better.

expression: kill two birds with one stone
translation: matar dois coelhos com uma cajadada só
definition_en: To accomplish two things at once
definition_pt: Realizar duas coisas ao mesmo tempo
example: I can shop and exercise - killing two birds with one stone.

definition_en: To avoid talking about past problems
definition_pt: Evitar falar sobre problemas passados
example: Don't bring up the argument - let sleeping dogs lie.

expression: like two peas in a pod
definition_en: Very similar to each other
definition_pt: Muito similares um ao outro
example: The twins are like two peas in a pod.

definition_en: To tell the main points without details
definition_pt: Contar os pontos principais sem detalhes
example: To make a long story short, we missed the flight.

definition_en: To have just enough money to survive
definition_pt: Ter dinheiro apenas o suficiente para sobreviver
example: With two jobs, he can barely make ends meet.

definition_en: To miss an opportunity
definition_pt: Perder uma oportunidade
example: I missed the boat on buying that cheap house.

expression: not playing with a full deck
definition_en: To be not very smart
definition_pt: Não ser muito inteligente
example: Sometimes I think he's not playing with a full deck.

definition_en: To be free from responsibility
definition_pt: Estar livre de responsabilidade
example: She let me off the hook and I didn't have to go.

definition_en: To be alert and aware
definition_pt: Estar alerta e consciente
example: You need to be on the ball in this job.

definition_en: Free, paid for by the business
definition_pt: Grátis, pago pelo estabelecimento
example: The drinks are on the house tonight.

definition_en: Something that is very easy to do
definition_pt: Algo que é muito fácil de fazer
example: The exam was a piece of cake.

expression: cost an arm and a leg
definition_en: To be very expensive
definition_pt: Ser muito caro
example: The new iPhone costs an arm and a leg.

definition_en: To study hard
definition_pt: Estudar muito
example: I need to hit the books before the final exam.

definition_en: Feeling ill or sick
definition_pt: Sentindo-se doente
example: I'm feeling a bit under the weather today.

definition_en: To face a difficult or unpleasant situation with courage
definition_pt: Enfrentar uma situação difícil ou desagradável com coragem
example: I decided to bite the bullet and go to the dentist.

translation: ações falam mais alto que palavras
definition_en: What you do is more important than what you say
definition_pt: O que você faz é mais importante do que o que você diz
example: Don't just promise to help - actions speak louder than words.

definition_en: Listening carefully and attentively
definition_pt: Ouvindo cuidadosa e atentamente
example: Tell me what happened - I'm all ears.

definition_en: Very expensive
definition_pt: Muito caro
example: The repairs cost me an arm and a leg.

definition_en: Someone you love very much
definition_pt: Alguém que você ama muito
example: My daughter is the apple of my eye.

expression: at the drop of a hat
definition_en: Immediately, without any hesitation
definition_pt: Imediatamente, sem qualquer hesitação
example: She would help me at the drop of a hat.

definition_en: To start again from the beginning
definition_pt: Começar novamente do início
example: The computer crashed, so I'm back to square one.

definition_en: Looking for something in the wrong place
definition_pt: Procurar algo no lugar errado
example: If you think I took your keys, you're barking up the wrong tree.

definition_en: To finish something before the deadline
definition_pt: Terminar algo antes do prazo
example: We worked hard to beat the clock and finish on time.

expression: beauty is in the eye of the beholder
translation: a beleza está nos olhos de quem vê
definition_en: Different people have different tastes
definition_pt: Pessoas diferentes têm gostos diferentes
example: I think it's ugly, but beauty is in the eye of the beholder.

definition_en: Avoiding the main point
definition_pt: Evitar o ponto principal
example: Stop beating around the bush and tell me the truth.

definition_en: To try very hard to help someone
definition_pt: Tentar muito ajudar alguém
example: She bent over backwards to make us comfortable.

expression: between a rock and a hard place
translation: entre a cruz e a espada
definition_en: In a difficult situation with no good options
definition_pt: Em uma situação difícil sem boas opções
example: I'm between a rock and a hard place with this decision.

expression: bite off more than you can chew
translation: pegar mais do que pode carregar
definition_en: To take on too much responsibility
definition_pt: Assumir responsabilidade demais
example: I bit off more than I could chew with this project.

definition_en: To release stress or energy
definition_pt: Liberar estresse ou energia
example: I go for a run to blow off steam.

definition_en: To be very expensive
definition_pt: Ser muito caro
example: This vacation won't break the bank.

expression: burn the candle at both ends
definition_en: To work too hard and get too little rest
definition_pt: Trabalhar demais e descansar pouco
example: He's burning the candle at both ends and getting sick.

definition_en: A nervous feeling before something important
definition_pt: Uma sensação de nervosismo antes de algo importante
example: I have butterflies in my stomach before the interview.

definition_en: To be in charge and make decisions
definition_pt: Estar no comando e tomar decisões
example: The CEO calls the shots around here.

definition_en: To be angry about something in the past
definition_pt: Estar irritado com algo do passado
example: He has a chip on his shoulder about not going to college.

definition_en: To become nervous before doing something
definition_pt: Ficar nervoso antes de fazer algo
example: She got cold feet and didn't go through with it.

expression: cross that bridge when you come to it
definition_en: To deal with a problem when it happens
definition_pt: Lidar com um problema quando ele acontecer
example: We'll cross that bridge when we come to it.

definition_en: To give a false alarm
definition_pt: Dar um alarme falso
example: If you keep crying wolf, nobody will believe you.

definition_en: To be less critical of someone
definition_pt: Ser menos crítico com alguém
example: Cut him some slack - he's new at this job.

definition_en: To think about pleasant things instead of working
definition_pt: Pensar em coisas agradáveis em vez de trabalhar
example: Stop daydreaming and finish your homework.

definition_en: Someone who argues against an idea for the sake of debate
definition_pt: Alguém que argumenta contra uma ideia para estimular o debate
example: I'll play devil's advocate and question your plan.

expression: don't count your chickens before they hatch
translation: não conte com o ovo antes da galinha botar
definition_en: Don't assume something will happen before it does
definition_pt: Não assuma que algo vai acontecer antes de acontecer
example: You might not win - don't count your chickens before they hatch.

expression: don't give up your day job
definition_en: You're not good enough to do this professionally
definition_pt: Você não é bom o suficiente para fazer isso profissionalmente
example: Your singing is okay, but don't give up your day job.

definition_en: To delay doing something
definition_pt: Atrasar ou adiar fazer algo
example: The government is dragging its feet on this issue.

definition_en: To fail to remember something
definition_pt: Falhar em lembrar de algo
example: I know his name, but I'm drawing a blank.

definition_en: Because of
definition_pt: Por causa de
example: The flight was delayed due to bad weather.

definition_en: Hard physical effort
definition_pt: Esforço físico intenso
example: This floor needs some elbow grease to get clean.

expression: every cloud has a silver lining
translation: toda nuvem tem um lado positivo
definition_en: There is something good in every bad situation
definition_pt: Há algo bom em toda situação ruim
example: Don't be sad - every cloud has a silver lining.

definition_en: To accept the negative consequences of your actions
definition_pt: Aceitar as consequências negativas de suas ações
example: I made a mistake, time to face the music.

definition_en: Annoyed or tired of something
definition_pt: Irritado ou cansado de algo
example: I'm fed up with your excuses.

definition_en: To feel slightly sick
definition_pt: Sentir-se ligeiramente doente
example: I think I'll stay home today, I feel under the weather.

definition_en: A small difference between two things
definition_pt: Uma pequena diferença entre duas coisas
example: There's a fine line between confidence and arrogance.

definition_en: To control your emotions
definition_pt: Controlar suas emoções
example: Get a grip and stop crying over nothing.

expression: get off on the wrong foot
definition_en: To start a relationship badly
definition_pt: Começar um relacionamento mal
example: We got off on the wrong foot, but now we're friends.

definition_en: To move past a problem or emotion
definition_pt: Superar um problema ou emoção
example: It happened a year ago - you need to get over it.

definition_en: To become more organized
definition_pt: Ficar mais organizado
example: You need to get your act together and study more.

expression: give the benefit of the doubt
definition_en: To believe someone's excuse without proof
definition_pt: Acreditar na desculpa de alguém sem provas
example: I'll give you the benefit of the doubt this time.

definition_en: To risk everything in an attempt to succeed
definition_pt: Arriscar tudo em uma tentativa de ter sucesso
example: We're going for broke and launching the product early.

definition_en: To take a risk
definition_pt: Correr um risco
example: I'll go out on a limb and say you'll get the job.

definition_en: Very jealous
definition_pt: Muito invejoso
example: I'm green with envy about your new car.

definition_en: A strong feeling about someone or something
definition_pt: Um forte pressentimento sobre alguém ou algo
example: I have a gut feeling that something is wrong.

definition_en: To persevere through a difficult time
definition_pt: Perseverar em um momento difícil
example: I know it's hard, but hang in there.

definition_en: To be very busy
definition_pt: Estar muito ocupado
example: I have my hands full with these three kids.

definition_en: To have a big success or win a lot of money
definition_pt: Ter um grande sucesso ou ganhar muito dinheiro
example: She hit the jackpot with that investment.

definition_en: In a very difficult situation
definition_pt: Em uma situação muito difícil
example: The company is in dire straits financially.

definition_en: Making a profit, not losing money
definition_pt: Tendo lucro, não perdendo dinheiro
example: Our business is finally in the black this year.

expression: hit the nail on the head
definition_en: To describe exactly what is causing a situation or problem
definition_pt: Descrever exatamente o que está causando uma situação ou problema
example: You hit the nail on the head with that analysis.

definition_en: Very rarely
definition_pt: Muito raramente
example: He visits his hometown once in a blue moon.

definition_en: To reveal a secret
definition_pt: Revelar um segredo
example: Don't spill the beans about the surprise party.

expression: let the cat out of the bag
definition_en: To reveal a secret by accident
definition_pt: Revelar um segredo acidentalmente
example: I let the cat out of the bag about the promotion.

translation: fazer algo de forma mais fácil e rápida
definition_en: To do something in the easiest or cheapest way
definition_pt: Fazer algo da maneira mais fácil ou barata
example: If you cut corners on safety, someone could get hurt.

definition_en: To start over because a plan failed
definition_pt: Recomeçar porque um plano falhou
example: Our idea didn't work, so it's back to the drawing board.

definition_en: To make a bad situation even worse
definition_pt: Tornar uma situação ruim ainda pior
example: He forgot my birthday, and to add insult to injury, he didn't even apologize.

definition_en: To go to bed
definition_pt: Ir para a cama
example: I'm tired, I'm going to hit the sack.

definition_en: To flatter someone to get something
definition_pt: Adular alguém para conseguir algo
example: He's trying to butter up the boss for a raise.

definition_en: To work late into the night
definition_pt: Trabalhar até tarde da noite
example: She burned the midnight oil to finish the project.

definition_en: A view from above or a general overview
definition_pt: Uma vista de cima ou uma visão geral
example: From the rooftop, you get a bird's eye view of the city.

definition_en: Someone who feels uncomfortable in a situation
definition_pt: Alguém que se sente desconfortável em uma situação
example: I felt like a fish out of water at the fancy party.

expression: a picture is worth a thousand words
translation: uma imagem vale mais que mil palavras
definition_en: Visual images are more powerful than words
definition_pt: Imagens visuais são mais poderosas que palavras
example: I won't explain it - a picture is worth a thousand words.

translation: ações falam mais alto que palavras
definition_en: What you do is more important than what you say
definition_pt: O que você faz é mais importante do que o que você diz
example: Don't just promise - actions speak louder than words.

expression: all that glitters is not gold
translation: nem tudo que reluz é ouro
definition_en: Things that look good aren't always good
definition_pt: Coisas que parecem boas nem sempre são boas
example: The job offer seems great, but all that glitters is not gold.

definition_en: Perfectly fine or in good condition
definition_pt: Perfeitamente bem ou em boas condições
example: After some rest, I'm as right as rain.

translation: não saber mais o que fazer
definition_en: To be completely frustrated and out of ideas
definition_pt: Estar completamente frustrado e sem ideias
example: I'm at my wits' end with this computer problem.

definition_en: In a difficult situation with few options
definition_pt: Em uma situação difícil com poucas opções
example: With my back against the wall, I had to accept their offer.

definition_en: To waste time on something already settled
definition_pt: Perder tempo com algo já resolvido
example: We already decided - stop beating a dead horse.

definition_en: Those in need must accept what they get
definition_pt: Aqueles que precisam devem aceitar o que recebem
example: I didn't like the hotel, but beggars can't be choosers.

expression: birds of a feather flock together
translation: cada qual se junta com seu igual
definition_en: Similar people tend to stay together
definition_pt: Pessoas semelhantes tendem a ficar juntas
example: They're both artists - birds of a feather flock together.

definition_en: To stop yourself from saying something
definition_pt: Parar de falar algo que quer dizer
example: I wanted to argue, but I bit my tongue.

definition_en: To report illegal or dishonest activity
definition_pt: Denunciar atividade ilegal ou desonesta
example: An employee blew the whistle on the fraud.

expression: born with a silver spoon in your mouth
definition_en: To be born into a wealthy family
definition_pt: Nascer em uma família rica
example: He never struggled - he was born with a silver spoon.

definition_en: To be too expensive or to ruin financially
definition_pt: Ser muito caro ou arruinar financeiramente
example: Dinner was expensive but didn't break the bank.

expression: burning the candle at both ends
translation: queimar a vela em ambas as pontas
definition_en: To work too hard and get too little rest
definition_pt: Trabalhar demais e descansar muito pouco
example: You'll get sick if you keep burning the candle at both ends.

expression: by the skin of your teeth
definition_en: Just barely succeeding
definition_pt: Conseguindo apenas por pouco
example: I passed the exam by the skin of my teeth.

definition_en: A difficult situation with no good solution
definition_pt: Uma situação difícil sem boa solução
example: I need experience to get a job, but I need a job to get experience - it's a catch-22.

definition_en: Very inexpensive
definition_pt: Muito barato
example: The tickets were cheap as chips.

definition_en: A fresh start without past mistakes
definition_pt: Um recomeço sem erros passados
example: After moving, I had a clean slate.

definition_en: Not clear at all; confusing
definition_pt: Nada claro; confuso
example: The instructions were as clear as mud.

expression: cost an arm and a leg
definition_en: To be extremely expensive
definition_pt: Ser extremamente caro
example: The repairs cost me an arm and a leg.

definition_en: To call for help when not needed
definition_pt: Pedir ajuda quando não é necessário
example: Stop crying wolf, or no one will believe you when it's real.

definition_en: To get to the point without wasting time
definition_pt: Ir direto ao ponto sem perder tempo
example: Let's cut to the chase - how much will it cost?

definition_en: A little-known person who surprises others
definition_pt: Uma pessoa pouco conhecida que surpreende os outros
example: She was a dark horse who won the competition.

expression: don't count your chickens before they hatch
translation: não conte com o ovo antes da galinha botar
definition_en: Don't assume success before it happens
definition_pt: Não assuma o sucesso antes de acontecer
example: You haven't won yet - don't count your chickens before they hatch.

expression: don't give up your day job
definition_en: You're not good enough to do this professionally
definition_pt: Você não é bom o suficiente para fazer isso profissionalmente
example: Your singing is okay, but don't give up your day job.

definition_en: To delay doing something
definition_pt: Atrasar ou adiar fazer algo
example: The government is dragging its feet on the decision.

definition_en: To eat a very large amount
definition_pt: Comer uma quantidade muito grande
example: After the game, he eats like a horse.

definition_en: Everyone gets a chance to succeed
definition_pt: Todo mundo tem a chance de ter sucesso
example: Don't worry - every dog has its day.

definition_en: Knowing someone too well can make you dislike them
definition_pt: Conhecer alguém muito bem pode fazer você não gostar dela
example: After ten years together, familiarity breeds contempt.

definition_en: To enrich yourself, often dishonestly
definition_pt: Enriquecer-se, frequentemente de forma desonesta
example: The politician used his position to feather his nest.

expression: get a taste of your own medicine
definition_en: To experience the same bad treatment you give
definition_pt: Experimentar o mesmo tratamento ruim que você dá
example: He was rude, and then someone was rude to him - he got a taste of his own medicine.

definition_en: To stop acting superior
definition_pt: Parar de agir como superior
example: Get off your high horse and listen to others.

definition_en: To become more organized
definition_pt: Ficar mais organizado
example: You need to get your act together and study more.

expression: give the benefit of the doubt
definition_en: To believe someone without proof
definition_pt: Acreditar em alguém sem provas
example: I'll give you the benefit of the doubt this time.

definition_en: To fail spectacularly
definition_pt: Fracassar de forma espetacular
example: His presentation went down in flames.

definition_en: To take a risk to help someone
definition_pt: Correr um risco para ajudar alguém
example: I'll go out on a limb and support your idea.

definition_en: To do what others are doing
definition_pt: Fazer o que os outros estão fazendo
example: I don't have a plan - I just go with the flow.

definition_en: To make something go more smoothly
definition_pt: Fazer algo correr mais suavemente
example: A small donation can grease the wheels at city hall.

definition_en: To accept something unpleasant without complaining
definition_pt: Aceitar algo desagradável sem reclamar
example: The wait is long, but we'll just grin and bear it.

definition_en: A strong instinct about something
definition_pt: Um forte instinto sobre algo
example: I have a gut feeling that something is wrong.

definition_en: To persevere and not give up
definition_pt: Perseverar e não desistir
example: I know it's hard, but hang in there.

definition_en: To be very busy with something
definition_pt: Estar muito ocupado com algo
example: I have my hands full with these three kids.

expression: hear something straight from the horse's mouth
definition_en: To hear information directly from the source
definition_pt: Ouvir informação diretamente da fonte
example: I heard it straight from the horse's mouth - he's quitting.

definition_en: To have a big success or win a lot
definition_pt: Ter um grande sucesso ou ganhar muito
example: She hit the jackpot with that investment.

definition_en: To wait and be patient
definition_pt: Esperar e ser paciente
example: Hold your horses! We're not ready yet.

definition_en: In a very difficult or dangerous situation
definition_pt: Em uma situação muito difícil ou perigosa
example: The company is in dire straits financially.

definition_en: In trouble
definition_pt: Em apuros
example: I'm in hot water with my boss for being late.

definition_en: Making a profit, not losing money
definition_pt: Tendo lucro, não perdendo dinheiro
example: Our business is finally in the black this year.

definition_en: To joke or tease someone
definition_pt: Brincar ou provocar alguém
example: Are you serious or are you pulling my leg?

definition_en: To take attention away from someone else's achievement
definition_pt: Tomar a atenção da conquista de outra pessoa
example: She announced her engagement at my wedding and stole my thunder.

definition_en: To become uncontrollable
definition_pt: Tornar-se incontrolável
example: The party got out of hand and the police were called.

definition_en: To quit something abruptly
definition_pt: Parar algo abruptamente
example: He went cold turkey and stopped smoking overnight.

definition_en: To accept the consequences of your actions
definition_pt: Aceitar as consequências das suas ações
example: You made a mistake, now you have to face the music.

definition_en: Something that will never happen
definition_pt: Algo que nunca vai acontecer
example: He'll clean his room when pigs fly.

translation: um mal que vem para o bem
definition_en: Something that seems bad but turns out to be good
definition_pt: Algo que parece ruim mas acaba sendo bom
example: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise.

definition_en: To avoid saying what you mean directly
definition_pt: Evitar dizer diretamente o que você quer dizer
example: Stop beating around the bush and tell me the truth.

definition_en: To be caught in the act of doing something wrong
definition_pt: Ser pego no ato de fazer algo errado
example: The thief was caught red-handed by the police.

definition_en: To give up; to admit defeat
definition_pt: Desistir; admitir derrota
example: After many failed attempts, he threw in the towel.

definition_en: Very common and not special
definition_pt: Muito comum e não especial
example: College graduates are a dime a dozen these days.

expression: a taste of your own medicine
definition_en: Having the same bad treatment you give to others
definition_pt: Receber o mesmo tratamento ruim que você dá aos outros
example: He was rude, so I gave him a taste of his own medicine.

translation: fala muito e não faz nada
definition_en: Promising to do things but never doing them
definition_pt: Prometer fazer coisas mas nunca fazê-las
example: Don't trust him - he's all talk and no action.

definition_en: An approximate estimate
definition_pt: Uma estimativa aproximada
example: Can you give me a ballpark figure of the cost?

definition_en: To avoid talking about the main topic
definition_pt: Evitar falar sobre o assunto principal
example: Stop beating around the bush and tell me the truth.

definition_en: Irrelevant or not important to the main topic
definition_pt: Irrelevante ou não importante para o assunto principal
example: That argument is beside the point entirely.

definition_en: Unacceptable or outside agreed standards
definition_pt: Inaceitável ou fora dos padrões acordados
example: His behavior was beyond the pale.

definition_en: To face a difficult situation bravely
definition_pt: Enfrentar uma situação difícil com bravura
example: I'll just have to bite the bullet and apologize.

definition_en: To become extremely angry
definition_pt: Ficar extremamente irritado
example: My mom will blow a fuse when she sees this mess.

definition_en: To innovate or do something original
definition_pt: Inovar ou fazer algo original
example: The scientist broke new ground with her discovery.

definition_en: To damage a relationship permanently
definition_pt: Danificar um relacionamento permanentemente
example: Don't burn bridges with your former employer.

definition_en: To disappoint someone by revealing the truth
definition_pt: Decepcionar alguém ao revelar a verdade
example: I hate to burst your bubble, but you didn't win.

definition_en: Being in charge and making decisions
definition_pt: Estar no comando e tomar decisões
example: The new manager is now calling the shots.

expression: caught between a rock and a hard place
translation: entre a cruz e a espada
definition_en: Stuck in a difficult situation with limited options
definition_pt: Preso em uma situação difícil com opções limitadas
example: I'm caught between a rock and a hard place.

definition_en: Trying to achieve something impossible
definition_pt: Tentar alcançar algo impossível
example: You're chasing rainbows if you think that will work.

definition_en: To remove tension by honest discussion
definition_pt: Remover a tensão através de uma discussão honesta
example: We need to clear the air about what happened.

definition_en: To return to the original situation after changes
definition_pt: Retornar à situação original após mudanças
example: After ten years, my career has come full circle.

definition_en: To hope for good luck
definition_pt: Esperar por boa sorte
example: I'm crossing my fingers that I get the job.

definition_en: To meet expectations or be good enough
definition_pt: Atender às expectativas ou ser bom o suficiente
example: He's a nice guy, but he can't cut the mustard.

definition_en: Completely dead or no longer working
definition_pt: Completamente morto ou não funcionando mais
example: The battery is dead as a doornail.

definition_en: To look at something twice because you're surprised
definition_pt: Olhar algo duas vezes porque está surpreso
example: I did a double take when I saw the price.

definition_en: Don't expect something to happen soon
definition_pt: Não espere que algo aconteça logo
example: He said he'll pay me back, but don't hold your breath.

definition_en: Until the very last moment
definition_pt: Até o último momento
example: The game was close right down to the wire.

definition_en: A very small amount compared to what is needed
definition_pt: Uma quantidade muito pequena comparada ao que é necessário
example: Our donation is just a drop in the bucket.

expression: fed up to the back teeth
definition_en: Completely annoyed or tired of something
definition_pt: Completamente irritado ou cansado de algo
example: I'm fed up to the back teeth with your excuses.

definition_en: To be practical and realistic
definition_pt: Ser prático e realista
example: She has her feet on the ground despite her success.

definition_en: Related by blood or human nature
definition_pt: Relacionado por sangue ou natureza humana
example: You can't abandon your own flesh and blood.

definition_en: To start discussing the important matters
definition_pt: Começar a discutir os assuntos importantes
example: Let's get down to brass tacks and talk about money.

expression: get the show on the road
definition_en: To start something or begin a journey
definition_pt: Começar algo ou iniciar uma jornada
example: Let's get the show on the road, we're late.

definition_en: To give permission to proceed
definition_pt: Dar permissão para prosseguir
example: The boss gave the green light to start production.

definition_en: To do something opposite to what is normal
definition_pt: Fazer algo oposto ao que é normal
example: His ideas go against the grain of traditional thinking.

definition_en: To become very angry or lose control
definition_pt: Ficar muito irritado ou perder o controle
example: He went off the deep end when he heard the news.

definition_en: To increase dramatically
definition_pt: Aumentar drasticamente
example: Prices have gone through the roof lately.

definition_en: To try something unlikely to solve a problem
definition_pt: Tentar algo improvável para resolver um problema
example: I'm grasping at straws for a solution.

expression: have your work cut out for you
definition_en: To have a difficult task ahead
definition_pt: Ter uma tarefa difícil pela frente
example: You have your work cut out for you with this project.

expression: if the shoe fits, wear it
definition_en: If a criticism applies to you, accept it
definition_pt: Se uma crítica se aplica a você, aceite-a
example: If the shoe fits, wear it - you are lazy.

definition_en: In trouble or in a difficult situation
definition_pt: Em apuros ou em uma situação difícil
example: I'm in hot water with my boss for being late.

definition_en: Losing money or having debt
definition_pt: Perdendo dinheiro ou tendo dívidas
example: The company has been in the red for two years.

definition_en: To join a popular trend or activity
definition_pt: Juntar-se a uma tendência ou atividade popular
example: Everyone is jumping on the plant-based diet bandwagon.

definition_en: To prevent something unpleasant from approaching
definition_pt: Prevenir que algo desagradável se aproxime
example: Vaccines help keep diseases at bay.

definition_en: To do something while waiting
definition_pt: Fazer algo enquanto espera
example: I'm just killing time until the movie starts.

definition_en: To search everywhere possible
definition_pt: Procurar em todos os lugares possíveis
example: The detective left no stone unturned.

expression: make a mountain out of a molehill
translation: transformar um problema pequeno em grande
definition_en: To exaggerate a small problem
definition_pt: Exagerar um pequeno problema
example: You're making a mountain out of a molehill.

definition_en: To cause trouble or change
definition_pt: Causar problemas ou mudanças
example: Don't make waves at the meeting.

definition_en: I won't tell anyone the secret
definition_pt: Não contarei o segredo a ninguém
example: Don't worry, my lips are sealed.

definition_en: You must suffer to achieve something
definition_pt: Você precisa sofrer para alcançar algo
example: I know training is hard, but no pain, no gain.

expression: off the top of my head
definition_en: Without thinking or checking first
definition_pt: Sem pensar ou verificar primeiro
example: Off the top of my head, I'd say about 50 people.

definition_en: To agree or understand something similarly
definition_pt: Concordar ou entender algo de forma semelhante
example: We need to get on the same page before we proceed.

definition_en: Unexpectedly, without warning
definition_pt: Inesperadamente, sem aviso
example: He called me out of the blue after years.

definition_en: To have an influence or be part of something
definition_pt: Ter influência ou fazer parte de algo
example: His attitude played a role in losing the client.

definition_en: To start something and proceed quickly and effectively
definition_pt: Começar algo e progredir rapidamente e efetivamente
example: The new employee hit the ground running on his first day.

definition_en: To avoid making a decision or choosing a side
definition_pt: Evitar tomar uma decisão ou escolher um lado
example: You can't sit on the fence forever; you need to decide.

expression: the ball is in your court
definition_en: It is your turn to take action or make a decision
definition_pt: É sua vez de agir ou tomar uma decisão
example: I've made my offer, now the ball is in your court.

definition_en: To get to the point without wasting time
definition_pt: Ir direto ao ponto sem perder tempo
example: Let's cut to the chase and discuss the main issue.

definition_en: Uncertain or undecided
definition_pt: Incerteza ou indeciso
example: Our vacation plans are still up in the air.

definition_en: To decide what to do as things happen
definition_pt: Decidir o que fazer conforme as coisas acontecem
example: We don't have a plan, we'll just play it by ear.

definition_en: To stop working for the rest of the day
definition_pt: Parar de trabalhar pelo resto do dia
example: We've done enough work; let's call it a day.

definition_en: To make a wrong assumption or direct efforts toward the wrong target
definition_pt: Fazer uma suposição errada ou direcionar esforços para o alvo errado
example: If you think I took your money, you're barking up the wrong tree.

definition_en: The worst possible situation that can happen
definition_pt: A pior situação possível que pode acontecer
example: The economic crisis created a perfect storm for small businesses.

translation: um mal que vem para bem
definition_en: Something that seems bad but turns out to be good
definition_pt: Algo que parece ruim mas acaba sendo bom
example: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise.

definition_en: An extremely difficult and demanding task
definition_pt: Uma tarefa extremamente difícil e exigente
example: Cleaning the entire house alone was a herculean task.

definition_en: A guess without any evidence
definition_pt: Um palpite sem nenhuma evidência
example: My answer was a shot in the dark, but I got it right.

definition_en: Someone who appears friendly but is dangerous
definition_pt: Alguém que parece amigável mas é perigoso
example: He seems kind, but he's a wolf in sheep's clothing.

definition_en: Suddenly became chaotic and out of control
definition_pt: Repentinamente tornou-se caótico e fora de controle
example: When the news spread, all hell broke loose.

definition_en: Always arguing in an aggressive way
definition_pt: Sempre discutindo de forma agressiva
example: The two politicians are at each other's throats.

definition_en: A difficult first experience in a new job or activity
definition_pt: Uma primeira experiência difícil em um novo trabalho ou atividade
example: My first day alone was a baptism of fire.

translation: enterrar o machado, fazer as pazes
definition_en: To end a quarrel and become friendly again
definition_pt: Terminar uma briga e tornar-se amigável novamente
example: After five years, they finally buried the hatchet.

definition_en: Very quickly and with great progress
definition_pt: Muito rapidamente e com grande progresso
example: Her English has improved by leaps and bounds.

definition_en: To speak frankly and directly
definition_pt: Falar francamente e diretamente
example: I like him because he calls a spade a spade.

definition_en: To be the first to criticize or accuse someone
definition_pt: Ser o primeiro a criticar ou acusar alguém
example: Let he who is without sin cast the first stone.

definition_en: To chat casually with someone
definition_pt: Conversar casualmente com alguém
example: We spent the afternoon chewing the fat.

definition_en: Impatient and eager to start something
definition_pt: Impaciente e ansioso para começar algo
example: The team was chomping at the bit to play.

definition_en: Almost successful, but not quite
definition_pt: Quase bem-sucedido, mas não completamente
example: Your guess was close but no cigar.

expression: cut off your nose to spite your face
translation: cortar o nariz para irritar o rosto
definition_en: To act out of revenge that ends up hurting yourself
definition_pt: Agir por vingança que acaba machucando a si mesmo
example: Don't quit just to upset them - that's cutting off your nose to spite your face.

expression: don't put all your eggs in one basket
translation: não colocar todos os ovos na mesma cesta
definition_en: Don't risk everything on a single venture
definition_pt: Não arrisque tudo em um único empreendimento
example: Invest in different things - don't put all your eggs in one basket.

definition_en: Very unlikely or impossible
definition_pt: Muito improvável ou impossível
example: Fat chance he'll apologize; he's too proud.

expression: fools rush in where angels fear to tread
translation: onde os anjos temem pisar, os tolos se apressam
definition_en: Inexperienced people try things that wise people avoid
definition_pt: Pessoas inexperientes tentam coisas que os sábios evitam
example: He invested without research - fools rush in where angels fear to tread.

definition_en: In almost every important way
definition_pt: Em quase todos os aspectos importantes
example: For all intents and purposes, the project is finished.

definition_en: From being very poor to being very rich
definition_pt: De ser muito pobre a ser muito rico
example: His story is a true from rags to riches tale.

expression: get your ducks in a row
definition_en: To get organized and prepared
definition_pt: Organizar-se e preparar-se
example: We need to get our ducks in a row before the audit.

definition_en: To give excuses instead of a direct answer
definition_pt: Dar desculpas em vez de uma resposta direta
example: Customer service gave me the runaround for an hour.

definition_en: To do everything that is possible
definition_pt: Fazer tudo que é possível
example: We went the whole nine yards for the wedding.

expression: have your head in the clouds
definition_en: To be unrealistic or daydreaming
definition_pt: Ser irrealista ou estar sonhando acordado
example: He has his head in the clouds and can't focus.

expression: if you can't stand the heat, get out of the kitchen
translation: se não suporta o calor, saia da cozinha
definition_en: If you can't handle pressure, leave
definition_pt: Se você não consegue lidar com pressão, saia
example: This job is stressful - if you can't stand the heat, get out of the kitchen.

expression: in the blink of an eye
definition_en: Very quickly
definition_pt: Muito rapidamente
example: The car disappeared in the blink of an eye.

definition_en: To go through many difficult steps
definition_pt: Passar por muitas etapas difíceis
example: I had to jump through hoops to get my visa.

expression: keep your nose to the grindstone
definition_en: To work hard without stopping
definition_pt: Trabalhar duro sem parar
example: She kept her nose to the grindstone and finished early.

expression: let the chips fall where they may
translation: deixe acontecer, seja o que Deus quiser
definition_en: To let things happen without worrying about results
definition_pt: Deixar as coisas acontecerem sem se preocupar com os resultados
example: I'll do my best and let the chips fall where they may.

expression: make hay while the sun shines
definition_en: To take advantage of a good opportunity
definition_pt: Aproveitar uma boa oportunidade
example: Business is good now, so make hay while the sun shines.

expression: no use crying over spilled milk
translation: não adianta chorar pelo leite derramado
definition_en: Don't be upset about things that can't be changed
definition_pt: Não fique chateado com coisas que não podem ser mudadas
example: It happened, so no use crying over spilled milk.

definition_en: Nervous and anxious
definition_pt: Nervoso e ansioso
example: I've been on pins and needles waiting for the results.

translation: cachorro queimado tem medo de água fria
definition_en: After a bad experience, you become more cautious
definition_pt: Após uma experiência ruim, você se torna mais cauteloso
example: I won't lend money again - once bitten, twice shy.

expression: out of the frying pan into the fire
translation: pular da frigideira para o fogo
definition_en: From a bad situation to a worse one
definition_pt: De uma situação ruim para uma pior
example: I left one bad job for an even worse one - out of the frying pan into the fire.

definition_en: To argue the opposite side for the sake of debate
definition_pt: Argumentar o lado oposto para estimular o debate
example: Let me play devil's advocate and question your idea.

definition_en: To postpone something
definition_pt: Adiar algo
example: We put the project on ice until next year.

definition_en: To set higher standards
definition_pt: Estabelecer padrões mais altos
example: Her performance raised the bar for everyone.

definition_en: To give a strong warning or scolding
definition_pt: Dar um aviso severo ou repreensão
example: The boss read him the riot act for being late.

definition_en: To be satisfied with past success and stop trying
definition_pt: Estar satisfeito com sucessos passados e parar de tentar
example: You can't rest on your laurels in this industry.

expression: see which way the wind blows
definition_en: To find out what people think before deciding
definition_pt: Descobrir o que as pessoas pensam antes de decidir
example: I'll see which way the wind blows before I vote.

definition_en: To go unnoticed or unattended
definition_pt: Passar despercebido ou desassistido
example: Some mistakes slip through the cracks despite our checks.

definition_en: To gain an advantage over someone by doing something before them
definition_pt: Ganhar vantagem sobre alguém fazendo algo antes
example: Our competitor stole a march on us with their new product.

definition_en: To take credit for someone else's achievement
definition_pt: Tomar o crédito pela conquista de outra pessoa
example: She announced her pregnancy at my wedding and stole my thunder.

expression: stick out like a sore thumb
definition_en: To be very obvious or noticeable in a negative way
definition_pt: Ser muito óbvio ou perceptível de forma negativa
example: In my formal suit, I stuck out like a sore thumb at the beach party.

expression: strike while the iron is hot
translation: bater enquanto o ferro está quente
definition_en: To take advantage of an opportunity immediately
definition_pt: Aproveitar uma oportunidade imediatamente
example: He's in a good mood - strike while the iron is hot and ask for a raise.

expression: take with a pinch of salt
definition_en: To not completely believe something
definition_pt: Não acreditar completamente em algo
example: Take his promises with a pinch of salt.

expression: the devil is in the details
definition_en: Small things can cause big problems
definition_pt: Pequenas coisas podem causar grandes problemas
example: The contract looks good, but the devil is in the details.

expression: the pot calling the kettle black
translation: o sujo falando do mal lavado
definition_en: Accusing someone of something you are also guilty of
definition_pt: Acusar alguém de algo que você também é culpado
example: You're criticizing my lateness? That's the pot calling the kettle black.

expression: two sides of the same coin
definition_en: Very closely related things that seem different
definition_pt: Coisas muito relacionadas que parecem diferentes
example: Genius and madness are two sides of the same coin.

expression: up a creek without a paddle
definition_en: In a difficult situation without solutions
definition_pt: Em uma situação difícil sem soluções
example: Without the car, we're up a creek without a paddle.

definition_en: An opportunity that is unlikely to happen again
definition_pt: Uma oportunidade que dificilmente acontecerá novamente
example: This trip to space is a once in a lifetime opportunity.

definition_en: The minimum number of people needed to operate
definition_pt: O número mínimo de pessoas necessárias para operar
example: The office is running with a skeleton crew during the holidays.

definition_en: An obvious problem that everyone ignores
definition_pt: Um problema óbvio que todos ignoram
example: We need to talk about the elephant in the room: our debt.

definition_en: Joking or teasing someone
definition_pt: Brincando ou provocando alguém
example: Don't believe him; he's pulling your leg.

expression: put your foot in your mouth
definition_en: To say something embarrassing or inappropriate
definition_pt: Dizer algo embaraçoso ou inapropriado
example: I really put my foot in my mouth when I mentioned her ex-boyfriend.

definition_en: To make more effort than expected
definition_pt: Fazer mais esforço do que o esperado
example: Our team always goes the extra mile for our clients.

definition_en: To agree with someone
definition_pt: Concordar com alguém
example: My boss and I don't always see eye to eye on everything.

definition_en: To hear rumors or informal information
definition_pt: Ouvir boatos ou informações informais
example: I heard on the grapevine that she's quitting.

expression: take with a grain of salt
definition_en: To regard something as exaggerated or not completely true
definition_pt: Considerar algo como exagerado ou não completamente verdadeiro
example: Take his stories with a grain of salt; he likes to exaggerate.
